reasoning: The FOMC meets on October 28-29, 2025. Leading up to the cutoff date of October 26, 2025, there is a very strong consensus for a 25 basis point rate cut. A Reuters poll published on October 21-22 showed that 115 out of 117 economists expect a 25 bps cut next week (search_0_result_3, search_1_result_0). Furthermore, the CME FedWatch tool was reported to be pricing in a 98% probability of a 25 bps cut (search_1_result_1). The Polymarket implied probability was roughly 95% around October 16. There are no news items or economic data releases from October 23 to October 26 indicating any sudden deviation from this broad consensus. The Fed is also in its pre-meeting blackout period, meaning no officials will speak to sway market expectations. Therefore, the probability of a 25 basis point reduction is exceptionally high. I am putting a 96% probability on this outcome, consistent with the futures market pricing and economist consensus.
